headline: Kelantan Refuses to Join National Water Commission for 18th Year
context: Kelantan has declined to participate in the National Water Services Commission (SPAN) framework since its establishment in 2008, maintaining independent control with the lowest service quality nationally.

cards.big: SPAN regulates water services in 12 states. Kelantan refuses to join.
cards.sub: Kelantan's non-revenue water rate is 58% — the worst in Malaysia. The national average is 35%.

cards.big: 58% of treated water in Kelantan is lost before reaching consumers.
cards.sub: Pipe replacement requires RM2.1B. AKSB cannot raise capital without federal-backed restructuring.

cards.big: Water supply interruptions in Kelantan averaged 147 hours per consumer in 2024.
cards.sub: The national average is 23 hours. Kelantan's rate is 6.4 times worse.

cards.big: Kelantan rejects SPAN on constitutional grounds under state water rights.
cards.sub: The legal argument is valid. The practical consequence is 1.9 million people with substandard water.

cards.big: Constitutional autonomy is important. So is clean water. Kelantan residents currently have one without the other.

The federal-state impasse on water must be resolved through a compromise that respects state rights while meeting service standards. Using people's water as a political bargaining chip is unconscionable.
